Examples.Title: Get the results of the painting
Examples.Description:
Inpaint, Outpaint, Smart Background and Expand services can work asynchronously and expose an inference identifier that can be used later to fetch te results.
Use this service to check up on the result.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Picsart;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get the Audio Generation result
Examples.Description: Use this method, along with inference id, to retrieve the generated speech or sound/music.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Picsart;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get the Logo Generator result
Examples.Description: Get the results of your logo generation. Use the inference identifier from the [Logo Generator](https://docs.picsart.io/reference/genai-generate-logo).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Picsart;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get the Text2Image result
Examples.Description: Get the generated images.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Picsart;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get the Text2Sticker result
Examples.Description: Get the generated stickers.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Picsart;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get the Video Generation result
Examples.Description: Use this method, along with inference id, to retrieve the generated video.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Picsart;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get the Design Import Result
Examples.Description: Get the result of the Design Import. Use the inference identifier from the POST request to fetch the latest status and result here.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Picsart;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Make Video CTV-Compatible
Examples.Description: Converts your original video to meet CTV requirements.
Examples.Code:
### Make Video CTV-Compatible
# @name video-encode-ctv
POST {{host}}/video/encode/ctv
X-Picsart-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "video_url": "https://example.com"
}

Examples.Title: Adjust Audio
Examples.Description: Adjusts the input video's audio volume, allows to add a new audio channel and control its volume.
Examples.Code:
### Adjust Audio
# @name video-adjust-audio
POST {{host}}/video/audio/adjust
X-Picsart-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "video_url": "https://example.com",
  "audio_url": "string",
  "audio_volume": 100,
  "video_volume": 100,
  "export": {
    "format": "MP4",
    "frame_rate": 30
  }
}

Examples.Title: Concatenate Videos and Images
Examples.Description: Concatenate several videos clips and images with transitions into one timeline. Helpful when adding intro/outro- or creating slideshows.
Examples.Code:
### Concatenate Videos and Images
# @name video-concat
POST {{host}}/video/concat
X-Picsart-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "items": [
    {
      "video_url": "https://example.com",
      "volume": 100,
      "image_url": "https://example.com",
      "scale": "fit",
      "duration": 1000,
      "transition": "none"
    }
  ],
  "bg_audio_url": "https://example.com",
  "bg_audio_repeat": true,
  "bg_audio_volume": 100,
  "export": {
    "format": "MP4",
    "frame_rate": 30
  }
}

Examples.Title: Create a highlights video from the original one. This picks highlight clips and concatenates them into one output video.
Examples.Description: Create a highlights video from the original one. This picks highlight clips and concatenates them into one output video.
Examples.Code:
### Create a highlights video from the original one. This picks highlight clips and concatenates them into one output video.

# @name video-concat-highlights
POST {{host}}/video/concat/highlights
X-Picsart-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "video_url": "https://example.com",
  "trim_segments": [
    {
      "start": 0,
      "end": 0,
      "transition": "none"
    }
  ],
  "export": {
    "format": "MP4",
    "frame_rate": 30
  }
}

Examples.Title: Edit Video
Examples.Description: Comprehensive editing with one operation. Apply effect, adjust, trim, crop, resize, change codecs, compression, framerates, etc.
Examples.Code:
### Edit Video
# @name video-edit
POST {{host}}/video/edit
X-Picsart-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "video_url": "https://example.com",
  "fit": {
    "ratio": 0,
    "width": 0,
    "height": 0,
    "bg_color": "string",
    "bg_blur": 0,
    "bg_image_url": "https://example.com",
    "bg_video_url": "https://example.com"
  },
  "crop": {
    "width": 0,
    "height": 0,
    "start_x": 0,
    "start_y": 0
  },
  "export": {
    "format": "MP4",
    "quality": "medium,",
    "codec": "default,",
    "audio_codec": "default,",
    "frame_rate": "30,",
    "color_space": "SRGB"
  }
}

Examples.Title: Adjust Video
Examples.Description:
Adjusts the input video file with 14 different options.
**Options:**
  * Adjust audio and/or volume
  * Adjust video volume
  * Adjust brightness and/or contrast
  * Adjust clarity and/or saturation
  * Adjust speed and/or reverse the video
  * Adjust hue and/or shadows
  * Adjust highlights and/or temperature
  * Adjust noise or sharpen the video
  * Adjust vignette

If you choose an adjustment setting value out of the allowed range, the default value of 0 is used instead.
Examples.Code:
### Adjust Video
# @name video-adjust
POST {{host}}/video/adjust
X-Picsart-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "video_url": "https://example.com",
  "audio_url": "string",
  "audio_volume": 100,
  "video_volume": 100,
  "brightness": 0,
  "contrast": 0,
  "clarity": 0,
  "saturation": 0,
  "hue": 0,
  "shadows": 0,
  "highlights": 0,
  "temperature": 0,
  "sharpen": 0,
  "noise": 0,
  "vignette": 0,
  "export": {
    "format": "MP4",
    "frame_rate": 30
  }
}

Examples.Title: Transcribe Audio
Examples.Description:
Transcribe your audio or video files. The input file can have one of the following mime types:
  * audio/flac
  * audio/mpeg
  * audio/x-m4a
  * audio/ogg
  * audio/x-wav
  * video/mp4
  * video/mpeg
  * video/webm
Note, that the maximum audio/video file size supported is 25mb.
Examples.Code:
### Transcribe Audio
# @name video-transcribe-audio
POST {{host}}/video/audio/transcribe
X-Picsart-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "file_url": "https://example.com",
  "language": "en",
  "format": "TXT",
  "granularity": "sentence"
}
